title: Open question on 1-Degreewidth complexity (Keeney and Lokshtanov)
statement_text: What is the computational complexity of deciding whether a digraph has degreewidth at most $1$?
context_text: Keeney and Lokshtanov [WG 2024] proved that deciding if a digraph has degreewidth $\leq 2$ is $\mathsf{NP}$-complete and left the complexity of the $k=1$ case open. This is the last unresolved case for oriented graphs, since $0$-Degreewidth (acyclicity) is trivially in $\mathsf{P}$.
paper_contribution: Proves that $1$-Degreewidth is $\mathsf{NP}$-complete, and more generally that $k$-Degreewidth is $\mathsf{NP}$-complete for every integer $k \geq 1$, settling the last open case for oriented graphs.
paper_title: Computing the degreewidth of a digraph is hard
